meta.title: carl — Roadmap
meta.blurb: A living plan for the game. We edit roadmap.json together: flip statuses, add items, revise decisions as we learn from playing. Keep it honest — if something's half-done or unproven, say so.
meta.vision: A classic fixed-path tower-defense game: enemies advance along set paths in waves, and you place & upgrade towers along them to stop them before they reach the goal. Built in Godot 4 (pivoted from an earlier 3D action prototype — same engine, rebuilt gameplay).
meta.note: Pre-prototype for the TD direction. Placeholder primitives only; reuse engine + tooling, rebuild the game.

sections.title: Next up
sections.desc: First TD prototype, roughly in build order.

sections.title: Completed
sections.desc: Shipped TD work, newest first. (Pre-pivot work is archived below.)

sections.title: Later
sections.desc: Bigger TD bets, not yet scheduled.

sections.title: Ideas & open questions
sections.desc: Directions under consideration, not yet committed.

sections.title: Known issues / debt
sections.desc: Things that work but aren't right yet.

sections.title: Archived — action prototype (pre-pivot)
sections.desc: Built before the tower-defense pivot. Kept for history; not the current game. Still in git + CHANGELOG.

decisions.text: PIVOT to tower defense — classic fixed-path TD (enemies on set paths, place towers along them). Replaces the 3D action/boss/puzzle direction.

Keep Godot + the engine/tooling (incl. the interactive roadmap); rebuild gameplay. Combat/level/boss systems are scrapped as the game, but health/projectile/enemy-movement code may be salvaged as reference.
Plan-first: this branch (plan-tower-defense) updates the roadmap only — no game code yet.
View-switching idea survives the pivot, reimagined for TD (tactical overview vs. zoomed-in). Still an idea, not committed.
[pre-pivot] 3D isometric first — to test the most ambitious action vision before simplifying.
[pre-pivot] Combat feel decided by playing — combat_mode switch; landed on ranged.
[pre-pivot] First-person added as a toggle, not a replacement.
Art deferred — placeholder shapes until the loop is proven fun. (Still holds for TD.)

items.title: Combat feedback: health bars, damage numbers, click-off dismiss
children: Enemy health bars (green over red): appear on damage/heal, hold 2s, fade 0.5s; any change resets the timer; billboard to camera. Reusable component for anything with a Health node.
Floating damage numbers (white, rounded, drift up + fade); beam's fractional DPS accumulates so it pops whole numbers
Dismiss the tower upgrade panel by clicking empty space or pressing Esc; clicking another tower switches

items.title: Bomb tower (4th type) + projectile speed
items.note: predictive AoE mortar that can whiff on turns
children: Lobs a bomb in an arc to a FIXED ground point = target pos + velocity × flight-time (lead prediction)
Landing point locks at fire time, so enemies that turn off their line get missed — intended weakness
AoE on landing with linear falloff (full at center → 40% at the edge → nothing outside); expanding blast visual
Projectile speed is now a per-tier stat on all towers and rises with upgrades; cannon/frost shots travel faster, and bomb speed tightens the lead
Data-driven via TYPES (bomb flag + aoe); HUD button/panel handle it automatically

items.title: Beam tower (3rd type)
items.note: continuous-DPS contrast to cannon burst / frost slow
children: Fires a solid beam at one in-range, in-LOS target; applies low damage continuously (DPS)
Instant retarget to the next valid enemy; single-target
Data-driven via TYPES (beam flag + dps tiers); HUD build button + upgrade panel pick it up automatically

items.title: Spherical range + line-of-sight + range preview
items.note: first step toward maps with verticality
children: Towers target within a true 3D (spherical) radius
Line-of-sight: a tower only fires at enemies it can see — raycast from the muzzle vs. blocker geometry (layer 4)
Demo obstacle (a raised wall) added to the map to show LOS blocking in action
Translucent range sphere/dome preview: shown while hovering a free slot (for the chosen type) and on a selected tower
Reusable hover-preview sphere in the controller; per-tower sphere shown on select

items.title: First playable TD prototype (core loop)
items.note: 3D iso, fixed path, fixed buildable slots — the full minimal loop is playable
children: L-shaped path via Path3D → waypoints; ground, slots, red goal; lit iso scene (reused)
Enemies walk the path (reuse health.gd); leaking costs a life, kills pay currency
Click a green slot to build a tower (costs currency); towers auto-target the leader & fire
Homing projectile so shots connect with moving creeps
Waves (5), economy (kills + start bank), 20 lives, win/lose, R to restart
HUD: lives / currency / wave + Start-wave button + messages; new td_main.tscn is the main scene

items.title: 2nd tower type + tiered upgrades + sell
items.note: the variety that makes it a game, not a tech demo
children: Frost tower: lower damage, slows enemies on hit (blue tint + slow-factor/duration); enemies have a slow effect
Two build-type buttons (Cannon / Frost) with costs; pick one, then click a slot
Tiered upgrades (Lv 1→3) via a click-to-select tower panel; stats + rising cost, capped at max
Sell a placed tower for 50% of total spent; frees the slot
Data-driven tower stats (TYPES table) so balancing/new tiers is a one-place edit

items.title: A 2nd enemy type
items.note: contrast the basic creep — e.g. fast-but-weak vs. slow-but-tanky; mix within a wave

items.title: Map verticality — multi-height terrain, elevated tower spots, paths that duck behind cover (LOS + spherical range groundwork is in)
Multiple maps / levels with different path layouts
More combat juice — hit particles, screen shake, death effects, SFX
More enemy types (armored, flying, shielded, boss waves)
Art pass — commit to a style once the loop is fun
View-switching as a core mechanic (reimagined for TD)
items.note: Carried over from the action design. The original idea — switch views on purpose and design around it — could fit TD in a fresh way. Still just an idea.
children: Tactical overview (iso/top-down) for placing & managing towers across the whole map…
…vs. a zoomed-in / first-person 'gunner' view where you manually aim or take control of a single tower for bonus effect during a wave.
Switch as a resource/decision: drop to the gunner view to clutch a leak, at the cost of overview of the rest of the map.
Open question: does manual control in a TD add depth or just busywork? Prototype one wave both ways before committing.
Keep parked until the plain fixed-path loop is proven fun.

items.title: Maze / free-placement TD twist
items.note: We chose classic fixed-path for the first prototype. If that proves fun, revisit letting players build the path with tower placement (Desktop TD / GemCraft style) for deeper strategy.

items.title: Old action scenes/scripts (player, boss, combat, level) still in the repo — decide per-file what to salvage vs. delete as TD takes shape.
In-app version overlay (version.gd) still reads as the action prototype's; revisit once TD has its own build.
Start-wave button is logically disabled mid-wave but doesn't look obviously disabled; could grey it out / add a 'wave in progress' state.
No tower range preview when placing; you can't see a tower's reach before/after building.
Interactive HTML roadmap (tooling — still in use)
children: JSON-backed roadmap web app with live editing saved to disk via a stdlib server
This tool survives the pivot; only the game direction changed

items.title: Weapons & FPS-style shooting
children: Body gun + first-person viewmodel; projectiles from the active muzzle; FP shots follow full 3D aim

items.title: First-person mode + presentation polish
children: FP toggle (V): mouse-look, camera-relative movement, camera-forward aim, crosshair
Lit environment (sky + ambient); per-instance enemy/boss materials

items.title: Level, platforming & enemies (action)
children: Linear level with arenas, platforming over pits, checkpoints
Chaser enemies; boss FSM (dormant → CHASE → TELEGRAPH → ATTACK → COOLDOWN); follow camera

items.title: Core combat prototype (action)
children: Iso movement + mouse aim; combat_mode melee/ranged/mixed; reliable projectile hits
HUD HP bars; reusable health.gd (candidate for TD reuse)
